title: Ironcrag Forge
description: A legendary smith keeps her mountain forge sealed against strangers. Tonight she is taking visitors, but the price she names is not gold.
author: AI

intro.passage: Ironcrag Forge squats against the cliff face, half-buried in snow. A bell hangs by a frayed rope beside the iron door. A wooden ladder leans against the wall, climbing toward the smoking chimney. A path of black slag winds around the back. No sign of the legendary smith who lives within.

choices.label: ring the bell
choices.hint: The bronze note rings flat against the cliff face and the wind grabs it.

choices.label: climb the ladder
choices.hint: Your boots find the rungs before your mind fully agrees with the plan.

choices.label: follow the path
choices.hint: Black slag crunches under your heel like broken pottery in a midden.

stone-steps.passage: The bell's last echo dies and the iron door swings wide. A bent woman in a leather apron stares you down. Her eyes are the color of hot iron. 'Brenna forges for none but the worthy,' she says. 'Enter if you dare. Or peer through the shutters at what awaits the unworthy.'

choices.label: enter the hall
choices.hint: Heat rolls out to meet you before you have crossed the threshold.

choices.label: peer through the shutters
choices.hint: The wood is warm and there are scratches along the inside of the slats.

cooling-yard.passage: Slag and ash crunch underfoot as you round the forge. A stone trough holds black water with a broken hammer at the bottom. The back door stands ajar, firelight spilling from within. Lower down, an iron grate vents steam from the underforge. Someone is humming in the dark below.

choices.label: open the back door
choices.hint: The hinges are oiled, you notice, as if someone uses them often.

choices.label: crawl through the grate
choices.hint: Steam beads on your face the moment you duck beneath the iron lip.

rooftop-ladder.passage: Rungs crack underhand as you climb. At the top, the chimney belches sulfur smoke and the roof tiles are warm against your boots. A skylight hatch slopes open. Through the gap, you see the forge floor glowing red below. A narrow gutter leads down the slate toward a side door.

choices.label: drop down the chimney
choices.hint: The rising heat braces against your soles like a cushion of hot air.

choices.label: slide off the roof
choices.hint: Slate scrapes your palms but the gutter holds your weight surprisingly well.

forge-hall.passage: The hall is vast and red with forge glow. Tools hang on every wall and an anvil the size of a wagon stands at the center. Brenna the Smith waits beside it, arms folded. A heavy book rests on a reading stand by the wall. The hearth roars like a living thing at the back.

choices.label: greet the smith
choices.hint: Her eyes lift to yours and something in her gaze narrows to a blade.

choices.label: inspect the old book
choices.hint: The binding is warm to the touch, warmer than the room around it.

choices.label: study the fire
choices.hint: A wave of heat presses against your cheeks and the air smells of iron.

brenna-greeting.passage: Brenna's grip is dry and warm. She tells you the forging has a price that gold cannot cover. She will take your service for a day, or she will send you below to her ledger keeper. Her eyes narrow. 'Choose now. The iron does not wait for soft hearts.'

choices.label: accept her terms
choices.hint: Her hand closes around yours and you cannot tell if she is smiling.

choices.label: bargain for coin
choices.hint: Her mouth turns in a way that does not quite reach her eyes.

old-ledger.passage: The book is bound in hide that feels too warm. Names fill the pages in rusted ink, thousands of them, each beside a blade's mark. A page near the back is blank. Behind the reading stand, a narrow set of stairs curls down into the floor. Smoke drifts up from below, carrying whispers.

choices.label: descend the stairs
choices.hint: The steps give beneath you with a groan and the whispers grow clearer.

choices.label: close the book
choices.hint: The pages snap shut but the whispers seem to linger a moment longer.

hearth-pit.passage: Heat rolls off the hearth in waves. Coals glow white under iron grates and the air smells of molten metal. A crucible bubbles on a hook above the flame. Beside it, a bronze bowl holds old smith-coins left as offerings. The fire hisses when you breathe, as if it knows you are there.

choices.label: stoke the coals
choices.hint: A sheet of sparks leaps for your face and you flinch just in time.

choices.label: toss in a coin
choices.hint: The copper vanishes into the flame without a sound, as if swallowed whole.

anvil-test.passage: Brenna hands you a hammer almost too heavy to lift. A glowing ingot sits on the anvil. 'Shape it into a leaf,' she says. 'Or any living thing you know.' Her gaze never leaves your hands. The hammer pulls at your wrist like a living weight. Sweat stings your eyes before the first swing.

choices.label: swing the hammer
choices.hint: The weight carries you forward and the ingot rings like a struck bell.

choices.label: eye the ingot
choices.hint: A thin seam runs through the glowing iron and catches your attention.

crucible-trial.passage: The crucible glows like a captive sun. Brenna appears at your shoulder with a pair of long tongs. 'Molten iron will not lie,' she says. 'If the pour is clean, the blade sings. If not, it screams.' A clay mold waits on the stone slab. Below it, bellows wheeze in a rhythm older than you are.

choices.label: pour the metal
choices.hint: Gold and red light spills across the slab and paints your hands copper.

choices.label: grip the crucible
choices.hint: The tongs tremble as you tighten your hold and the heat crawls up the handle.

crooked-stairs.passage: The stairs creak downward into a low vault. A single candle gutters on a rough desk. An open guest book lies beside a quill dipped in red ink. Past the desk, a plain door has no handle, only a faint seam in the rock. The air smells of iron and something older.

choices.label: sign the guest book
choices.hint: The quill feels heavier than it should, as if it knows your hand.

choices.label: press the seam
choices.hint: The rock is cool beneath your palm and gives inward with a soft hiss.

name-register.passage: The quill trembles in its ink pot, waiting. Other names are scrawled above the empty line, some in shaky hand, some in childish print. At the bottom, in fresh rust-red, someone has written the name of a merchant you saw hanged two winters ago. The candle gutters as you lean in.

choices.label: write your true name
choices.hint: The letters flow from the nib before you decide what they should be.

choices.label: leave the page blank
choices.hint: Your hand lifts away and the quill drips a single red bead onto the wood.

secret-door.passage: The seam parts with a grinding breath of stale air. Beyond, a narrow passage glows faintly from crystals in the walls. A low hum rises, like a choir just out of hearing. At the far end, something metal gleams on a black pedestal. Footsteps approach from the vault behind you.

choices.label: sprint to the pedestal
choices.hint: The crystals pulse brighter as you cross the passage, as if feeding on haste.

choices.label: retreat up the stairs
choices.hint: The humming fades behind you but something has noticed you were there.

blade-vault.passage: The pedestal holds a blade like no other you have seen. Its edge drinks the faint crystal light. Another sword rests on a lower shelf, plainer but still finely made. Behind the pedestal, rune marks etched into the wall pulse slowly. The humming grows louder. Your hand is already moving forward.

choices.label: grab the legendary sword
choices.hint: The hilt is heavier than any blade you have ever lifted, and it hums softly.

choices.label: take the plain blade
choices.hint: The steel feels honest in your hand and that alone is worth something.

tool-rack.passage: Brenna nods at your first strike and points to a wall of tools. Silver tongs and a plain iron hammer hang side by side. She says the choice matters more than the swing. A furnace at the end of the hall roars hotter, waiting for its master. You smell sulfur and burned leather.

choices.label: claim the silver tongs
choices.hint: The silver is unmarked, unused, waiting for a hand that knows what to do.

choices.label: lift the iron hammer
choices.hint: The handle is wrapped in leather darkened by a thousand sweaty palms before yours.

bellows-room.passage: The bellows chamber is a cramped loft above the hearth. Two great leather sacks hang from the rafters, connected to ropes that vanish into the forge below. A back window lets in a knife of cold mountain air. Soot coats everything. Something scratches at the inside of the furthest bellows, slow and patient.

choices.label: pump the ropes
choices.hint: The sacks fill and empty with a rhythm that matches your own breathing.

choices.label: slip out the window
choices.hint: Mountain wind hits your face like a promise of freedom you cannot refuse.

final-forge.passage: The blade lies red-hot on the anvil, ready for its final bite. Two vessels wait beside it, an iron bucket of black oil and a clay jar of dark blood. Brenna watches without a word. The metal sings as it waits. Your next move decides what kind of sword walks out of this forge.

choices.label: quench in the oil
choices.hint: The oil surface is black and still and waits for your decision without judgment.

choices.label: plunge in the blood
choices.hint: A coppery scent rises from the clay jar and you feel it reach for you.

ending-masterblade.passage: The oil swallows the blade in a plume of black smoke. When you lift it, the steel is perfect, clean and singing in your hand. Brenna places a calloused palm on your shoulder. 'Take it,' she says. 'And remember that mercy weighs more than any blade.' You step into the snow with a legend at your hip.

ending.title: The Master's Blade
ending.flavor: Legends are forged in patience, not in blood.

ending-cursed-blade.passage: Steam screams from the clay jar as the blade drinks deep. The metal darkens, then flushes a deep red. Brenna steps back and will not meet your eyes. 'You have made a hungry thing,' she says quietly. 'Feed it well, or it will feed itself.' The sword hums with an eager voice as you carry it away.

ending.title: The Hungry Edge
ending.flavor: Some blades make promises their wielders cannot keep.

ending-lost-name.passage: The quill moves as if pulled by your own bones. When the last letter is dry, you cannot remember the name you just wrote. The candle flares once and goes still. Upstairs, Brenna is writing it into her own book, adding a fresh mark beside it. You walk out into the pass, and no one ever calls you that word again.

ending.title: The Stolen Name
ending.flavor: A name given in trade is a name forever lost.

ending-thief.passage: The legendary sword is lighter than it looks. Alarms ring somewhere distant as you sprint for the stairs. Brenna's voice follows you through the vaults, more sad than angry. You burst into the snowy pass clutching a blade worth a kingdom, with every bounty hunter in three realms soon to be on your trail. Freedom has a jagged edge.

ending.title: The Thief's Reward
ending.flavor: A stolen blade cuts its wielder first.

ending-escape.passage: Wind slams the back window shut behind you. The pass stretches down the mountain, bright with moonlight and untouched snow. Brenna does not call you back. Whatever you hoped to find in the forge, it was not meant for you, and that knowledge sits lighter than any sword. The road home is long, but you will walk it warm.

ending.title: The Long Walk Home
ending.flavor: Some doors are better left closed behind us.
